name: ai-to-aws
description: End-to-end AI migration to Amazon Bedrock: assess your codebase, design the architecture, then execute — rewriting SDK calls, evaluating quality, and delivering a ready-to-merge git branch. Requires the migration-to-aws plugin (it powers the Assess phase).

interface.shortDescription: Migrate OpenAI/Gemini/Anthropic workloads to Amazon Bedrock: rewrite SDK calls, evaluate quality, deliver a ready-to-merge branch.
interface.longDescription:
Point this plugin at a git repository that calls the OpenAI, Gemini, or Anthropic SDK. It assesses your AI workloads (delegating to the migration-to-aws plugin), then executes the migration: rewriting SDK calls to the Bedrock Converse API on a dedicated git branch, building a golden dataset from your production logs, evaluating output quality against Bedrock with LLM-as-judge scoring, migrating your existing tests, and producing a migration report with cost comparison and per-prompt quality scores.

Every phase checkpoints to disk, so interrupted runs resume without re-paying for completed evaluation calls. User-visible behavior differences (e.g. temperature ranges) are surfaced as explicit decisions, never silently changed.

Note: on platforms without subagent dispatch, the plugin runs in inline mode — fully functional but slower, with a mandatory checkpoint between phases.
defaultPrompt: Migrate my OpenAI app to Amazon Bedrock
Migrate my Gemini app to Amazon Bedrock
Move my Anthropic API calls to Bedrock
Rewrite my LLM SDK calls for Bedrock and evaluate quality
